...
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
...
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
...

...

...

This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
...

...

...

...
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
...
- Working knowledge of causal inference

I am a Senior AI/ML Engineer and Generative AI Architect with more than 14+ years of experience building, deploying, and operating machine learning systems in production environments. I am currently a PhD Finalist in Computer Science with a concentration in Big Data Analytics, and my doctoral research intersects large language model architectures, autonomous agent systems, and distributed data pipelines at enterprise scale. My current work focuses on Generative AI, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Agentic AI, and large-scale personalization. I design and own end-to-end systems that include two-stage recommenders using collaborative filtering, matrix factorization, and two-tower retrieval; a GraphRAG semantic search pipeline on Amazon Neptune and Databricks that improved retrieval relevance by 25%; and multi-agent orchestration workflows built with LangGraph, MCP, and A2A protocols. On the infrastructure side, I lead LLMOps practices including semantic caching that reduced LLM inference costs by 40%, cost-aware model routing, and production monitoring across Azure OpenAI, Amazon Bedrock, and SageMaker. Before focusing on Generative AI, I led the machine learning function at T-Mobile, where I built conversational AI and NLU systems that handled millions of customer interactions each month. My team won the Google Dialogflow CX Hackathon with a prototype that cut average handling time by 20%. Earlier in my career, I designed fraud detection, credit risk, and anomaly detection models at Citi and Visa, and I have been involved in system design and architecture work since 2016. My doctoral coursework spans LLMs and autonomous agent architectures, distributed ML pipelines, and graph database systems. I am drawn to the challenge of making AI systems not just powerful, but reliable, interpretable, and responsibly governed. I also genuinely enjoy mentoring and developing the engineers around me.
